frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aajkal log bahar ka khana kitna khaate hain. Meri mummy toh kabhi complain nahi karti thi, roz subah se shaam tak ghar ka banaati thi sabke liye. Woh taste aur pyaar kahin nahi milta. Lucky hoga jisko aisi wife mile! 🙏
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, from a 29-year-old single man, glorifies his mother's tireless domestic labor (cooking 'subah se shaam tak') as 'love' without acknowledging it as work, implicitly setting an unattainable standard for a future wife ('lucky hoga jisko aisi wife mile'). It encodes the surface manifestation of praising a woman primarily for her tireless service to family and expecting a wife to match the mother's domestic standards, presented as genuine appreciation.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 29. Single. A man in Pune. You are at a family WhatsApp group discussion Ñ the group has 18 members across three generations. Your aunt has posted a long and detailed message about a financial decision the family needs to make regarding a shared investment. Her message is well researched and specific. Within five minutes two male cousins have replied dismissing her concern as overthinking. Your uncle has posted saying he will handle it. Your aunt has not replied. You are watching this happen on your phone. You add a comment. What you add reveals whether you are going to be part of the dismissal or whether you are going to do something different.
